title: Emit 3-topic events (Transfer, Approval) directly via log3 opcode without ABI encoding
description: Solidity's emit keyword ABI-encodes non-indexed parameters and manages memory allocation. Using log3 directly writes the non-indexed data (e.g. amount) to a known memory location and passes the three indexed topics (event signature + two addresses) as stack arguments. Avoids memory allocation, ABI encoding overhead, and free pointer updates.
pattern_before:
emit Transfer(msg.sender, to, amount);
// Solidity generates: abi.encode(amount), allocate memory, log3
pattern_after:
mstore(0x20, amount)
log3(0x20, 0x20, _TRANSFER_EVENT_SIGNATURE, caller(), shr(96, mload(0x0c)))
when_to_apply: Any event with exactly 3 topics (signature + 2 indexed) and one non-indexed uint256 parameter
when_not_to_apply: Events with dynamic-length non-indexed data (bytes, string), events requiring ABI encoding of structs
